rules: Before entering any swing position, confirm at least one binary catalyst (earnings release, contract award, analyst event, or product announcement) is scheduled within the 10-day hold window; if no such catalyst exists, place the idea on the watchlist only and do not open a position.
Set a hard stop-loss order at exactly 3.5% below entry price for every swing position at the moment the fill is confirmed, without exception and without discretionary override.
Cap total exposure to any single foreign country across all portfolios at 15% of combined portfolio value, and permit no more than one China-related position in the Core portfolio at any time.

Never classify a position as Core unless it meets all three criteria simultaneously: minimum average daily US trading volume of $10M, primary listing on a major US exchange (not OTC), and trailing 90-day beta below 1.3; any position failing one or more thresholds must be labeled Speculative at entry.

Do not hold OTC-listed foreign ADRs, including Chinese ADRs and quasi-government foreign bank ADRs, in any portfolio under any classification due to liquidity risk, regulatory overhang, and forced delisting exposure.

Before entering any stock with a price-to-sales ratio above 10x or average daily US volume below $5M, confirm in writing that VIX is below 20 and the relevant sector ETF is trading above its 20-day moving average; if either condition fails, reduce intended position size by 50% or stand aside entirely.

Swing trades on large-cap software or high-multiple growth stocks require a confirmed earnings date or product announcement within the hold window as the entry trigger; sector momentum or multi-quarter thematic narrative alone is not a sufficient entry condition.
Government IT contract momentum trades require a confirmed contract award announcement or earnings beat as the entry trigger; sector sentiment without a discrete near-term event does not qualify.

Before entering any swing trade, verify that the broader market VIX is below 20 and the relevant sector ETF is trending above its 20-day moving average; if either condition is not met, stand aside regardless of individual stock thesis quality.

Maintain at least one regulated utility or defensive sector position in every Core portfolio at all times to offset high-beta or macro-sensitive holdings; rate case decisions and earnings from regulated utilities qualify as reliable, low-competition catalysts.

Prioritize domestic consumer brand leaders with demonstrable pricing power and clear near-term demand evidence as swing trade candidates over foreign macro recovery narratives; these structures are more reliably actionable within a 10-day hold window.

High-multiple growth stocks such as those with price-to-sales above 10x require a defined technical breakout confirmation or an imminent catalyst as entry criteria; momentum alone without a structural entry trigger is prohibited as the sole basis for entry.

momentum.rationale: Momentum-only entries without catalyst confirmation were the primary driver of losses across NET, CRM, CACI, and TCOM; momentum remains a supporting filter but is no longer sufficient as a standalone entry basis.

mean_reversion.rationale: Mean reversion strategies in macro-sensitive or illiquid names like IDCBY and CIHKY produced asymmetric downside; the factor is deprioritized until liquidity and regime filters are consistently applied.

value.rationale: Value remains relevant for Core portfolio construction, particularly in defensive and utility sectors, but must be paired with liquidity and exchange-listing criteria to avoid value traps in illiquid foreign names.

event_driven.rationale: Event-driven positioning is the highest-conviction factor given that confirmed near-term catalysts are now mandatory for all swing entries; this factor should dominate swing allocation decisions and is the primary differentiator between actionable ideas and watchlist candidates.

sector_rotation.rationale: Sector rotation remains useful for identifying macro regime alignment and sector ETF trend confirmation, which is now a required pre-entry check, but should not drive position sizing or entry timing without a discrete catalyst present.
